matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 所以我作為健康心理學家的目標改變了。我不再想擺脫你的壓力。我想讓你更好地應對壓力。我們只是做了一點幹預。如果你舉手說你去年壓力很大，我們本來可以救你的命，

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: because hopefully the next time your heart is pounding from stress, you're going to remember this talk and you're going to think to yourself, this is my body helping me rise to this challenge. And when you view stress in that way, your body believes you, and your stress response becomes healthier.
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 因為希望下次當你因壓力而心跳加速時，你會記住這次演講，並且你會想，這是我的身體在幫助我應對這一挑戰。當你以這種方式看待壓力時，你的身體就會相信你，你的壓力反應就會變得更健康。

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: Now I said I have over a decade of demonizing stress to redeem myself from, so we are going to do one more intervention. I want to tell you about one of the most under-appreciated aspects of the stress response, and the idea is this: Stress makes you social. To understand this side of stress,
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 現在我說我有十多年的妖魔化壓力需要救贖自己，所以我們將再進行一次幹預。我想告訴你壓力反應中​​最被低估的方面之一，其想法是：壓力讓你變得社交。要了解壓力的這一面，

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: we need to talk about a hormone, oxytocin, and I know oxytocin has already gotten as much hype as a hormone can get. It even has its own cute nickname, the cuddle hormone, because it's released when you hug someone. But this is a very small part of what oxytocin is involved in.
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 我們需要談談一種激素，即催產素，我知道催產素已經得到了與激素一樣多的炒作。它甚至還有一個可愛的綽號——擁抱荷爾蒙，因為當你擁抱某人時，它就會釋放出來。但這只是催產素所涉及的一小部分。

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: Oxytocin is a neuro-hormone. It fine-tunes your brain's social instincts. It primes you to do things that strengthen close relationships. Oxytocin makes you crave physical contact with your friends and family. It enhances your empathy. It even makes you more willing to help and support
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 催產素是一種神經激素。它可以微調您大腦的社交本能。它促使你去做一些能夠加強親密關係的事情。催產素會讓您渴望與朋友和家人有身體接觸。它增強了你的同理心。它甚至讓你更願意提供幫助和支持

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: the people you care about. Some people have even suggested we should snort oxytocin... to become more compassionate and caring. But here's what most people don't understand about oxytocin. It's a stress hormone. Your pituitary gland pumps this stuff out as part of the stress response.
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 你關心的人。有些人甚至建議我們應該吸食催產素…以變得更有同情心和關懷。但這是大多數人對催產素不了解的地方。這是一種壓力荷爾蒙。作為壓力反應的一部分，你的腦下垂體會泵出這些物質。

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: It's as much a part of your stress response as the adrenaline that makes your heart pound. And when oxytocin is released in the stress response, it is motivating you to seek support. Your biological stress response is nudging you to tell someone how you feel, instead of bottling it up.
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 它與讓你心跳加速的腎上腺素一樣，都是壓力反應的一部分。當催產素在壓力反應中​​釋放時，它會激勵您尋求支持。你的生物壓力反應會促使你告訴別人你的感受，而不是壓抑它。

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: Your stress response wants to make sure you notice when someone else in your life is struggling so that you can support each other. When life is difficult, your stress response wants you to be surrounded by people who care about you.
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 您的壓力反應希望確保您注意到生活中其他人正在掙扎，以便您可以互相支持。當生活困難時，你的壓力反應希望你周圍都是關心你的人。

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: Okay, so how is knowing this side of stress going to make you healthier? Well, oxytocin doesn't only act on your brain. It also acts on your body, and one of its main roles in your body is to protect your cardiovascular system from the effects of stress. It's a natural anti-inflammatory.
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 好吧，那麼了解壓力的這一面如何讓你更健康呢？嗯，催產素不只作用於你的大腦。它還作用於您的身體，它在您體內的主要作用之一是保護您的心血管系統免受壓力的影響。它是一種天然的抗發炎藥。

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: It also helps your blood vessels stay relaxed during stress. But my favorite effect on the body is actually on the heart. Your heart has receptors for this hormone, and oxytocin helps heart cells regenerate and heal from any stress-induced damage. This stress hormone strengthens your heart.
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 它還可以幫助您的血管在壓力下保持放鬆。但我最喜歡的對身體的影響其實是對心臟的影響。您的心臟有這種激素的受體，催產素可以幫助心臟細胞再生並治癒任何壓力引起的損傷。這種壓力荷爾蒙可以增強你的心臟。

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: And the cool thing is that all of these physical benefits of oxytocin are enhanced by social contact and social support. So when you reach out to others under stress, either to seek support or to help someone else, you release more of this hormone, your stress response becomes healthier,
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 最酷的是，催產素的所有這些身體益處都可以透過社交接觸和社會支持來增強。因此，當您在壓力下向他人伸出援手時，無論是尋求支持還是幫助他人，您都會釋放更多這種激素，您的壓力反應會變得更健康，

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: and you actually recover faster from stress. I find this amazing, that your stress response has a built-in mechanism for stress resilience, and that mechanism is human connection. I want to finish by telling you about one more study. And listen up, because this study could also save a life.
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 事實上，你從壓力中恢復得更快。我發現這很神奇，你的壓力反應有一個內建的壓力恢復機制，而這個機制就是人際關係。最後我想向大家介紹另一項研究。聽著，因為這項研究也可以救命。

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: This study tracked about 1,000 adults in the United States, and they ranged in age from 34 to 93, and they started the study by asking, "How much stress have you experienced in the last year?" They also asked, "How much time have you spent helping out friends, neighbors, people in your community?"
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 這項研究追蹤了大約 1000 名美國成年人，他們的年齡從 34 歲到 93 歲不等，他們在研究開始時詢問“你去年經歷了多少壓力？”他們還問：“你花了多少時間幫助朋友、鄰居和社區裡的人？”

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: And then they used public records for the next five years to find out who died. Okay, so the bad news first: For every major stressful life experience, that increased the risk of dying by 30 percent. But -- and I hope you are expecting a "but" by now -- but that wasn't true for everyone.
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 然後他們利用接下來五年的公開記錄來找出死亡人數。好的，首先是壞消息：每經歷一次重大壓力生活經歷，死亡風險就會增加 30%。但是——我希望你現在正在期待一個「但是」——但不是每個人都這樣。

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: People who spent time caring for others showed absolutely no stress-related increase in dying. Caring created resilience. And so we see once again that the harmful effects of stress on your health are not inevitable. How you think and how you act can transform your experience of stress.
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 花時間照顧他人的人絕對沒有表現出與壓力相關的死亡增加。關懷創造了韌性。因此，我們再次看到壓力對健康的有害影響並非不可避免。您的思維方式和行為方式可以改變您的壓力體驗。

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: When you choose to view your stress response as helpful, you create the biology of courage. And when you choose to connect with others under stress, you can create resilience. Now I wouldn't necessarily ask for more stressful experiences in my life,
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 當你選擇將你的壓力反應視為有幫助時，你就創造了勇氣的生物學。當您選擇在壓力下與他人聯繫時，您可以創造韌性。現在我不一定會要求生活中有更多壓力的經歷，

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: but this science has given me a whole new appreciation for stress. Stress gives us access to our hearts. The compassionate heart that finds joy and meaning in connecting with others, and yes, your pounding physical heart, working so hard to give you strength and energy.
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 但這項科學讓我對壓力有了全新的體會。壓力讓我們觸及內心。富有同情心的心在與他人的連結中找到快樂和意義，是的，你的心臟劇烈跳動，並努力工作給你力量和能量。

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: And when you choose to view stress in this way, you're not just getting better at stress, you're actually making a pretty profound statement. You're saying that you can trust yourself to handle life's challenges. And you're remembering that you don't have to face them alone.
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 當你選擇以這種方式看待壓力時，你不僅會更好地應對壓力，而且實際上會做出非常深刻的陳述。你是說你可以相信自己能夠應付生活中的挑戰。而且你要記住，你不必獨自面對它們。

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: Chris Anderson: This is kind of amazing, what you're telling us. It seems amazing to me that a belief about stress can make so much difference to someone's life expectancy. How would that extend to advice, between, say, a stressful job and a non-stressful job, does it matter which way they go?
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 克里斯安德森：你告訴我們的這有點令人驚奇。對我來說，令人驚訝的是，關於壓力的信念可以對一個人的預期壽命產生如此大的影響。這將如何延伸到建議，比如說，在有壓力的工作和無壓力的工作之間，他們走哪條路很重要嗎？

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: It's equally wise to go for the stressful job so long as you believe that you can handle it, in some sense? KM: Yeah, and one thing we know for certain is that chasing meaning is better for your health than trying to avoid discomfort. And so I would say that's really the best way to make decisions,
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 從某種意義上說，只要您相信自己能夠處理好，從事有壓力的工作同樣是明智的？ KM：是的，我們確信的一件事是，追逐意義比試圖避免不適更有利於你的健康。所以我想說這確實是做出決定的最佳方式，

matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.en: is go after what it is that creates meaning in your life and then trust yourself to handle the stress that follows. CA: Thank you so much, Kelly. It's pretty cool. Notifications alt+T
matt_cutts_try_something_new_for_30_days.zh: 就是追求那些為你的生活創造意義的東西，然後相信自己能夠處理隨之而來的壓力。 CA：非常感謝你，凱利。非常酷。通知 alt+T

angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.en: A few years ago, I felt like I was stuck in a rut, so I decided to follow in the footsteps of the great American philosopher, Morgan Spurlock, and try something new for 30 days. The idea is actually pretty simple. Think about something you've always wanted to add to your life
angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.zh: 幾年前，我覺得自己陷入了墨守成規的境地，所以我決定追隨偉大的美國哲學家摩根·斯普爾洛克的腳步，用30天的時間嘗試一些新的東西。這個想法其實非常簡單。想想你一直想為你的生活添加一些東西

angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.en: and try it for the next 30 days. It turns out 30 days is just about the right amount of time to add a new habit or subtract a habit -- from your life. There's a few things I learned while doing these 30-day challenges. instead of the months flying by, forgotten, the time was much more memorable.
angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.zh: 並在接下來的 30 天內嘗試。事實證明，30 天正好是在你的生活中添加一個新習慣或減少一個習慣的合適時間。在進行這 30 天的挑戰時，我學到了一些東西。與其說幾個月的時間飛逝而過、被遺忘，不如說那段時光更令人難忘。

angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.en: This was part of a challenge I did to take a picture every day for a month. And I remember exactly where I was and what I was doing that day. that as I started to do more and harder 30-day challenges, my self-confidence grew. I went from desk-dwelling computer nerd
angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.zh: 這是我在一個月內每天拍照的挑戰的一部分。我清楚地記得那天我在哪裡以及我在做什麼。當我開始進行更多、更難的 30 天挑戰時，我的自信心增強了。我從坐在辦公桌前的電腦迷變成了

angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.en: to the kind of guy who bikes to work. Even last year, I ended up hiking up Mt. Kilimanjaro, the highest mountain in Africa. I would never have been that adventurous before I started my 30-day challenges. I also figured out that if you really want something badly enough,
angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.zh: 對於那種騎自行車上班的人。甚至去年，我就徒步登上了非洲最高峰乞力馬扎羅山。在開始 30 天的挑戰之前，我絕對不會如此富有冒險精神。我還發現，如果你真的很想要某樣東西，

angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.en: you can do anything for 30 days. Have you ever wanted to write a novel? Every November, tens of thousands of people try to write their own 50,000-word novel, from scratch, It turns out, all you have to do is write 1,667 words a day By the way, the secret is not to go to sleep
angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.zh: 30天內你可以做任何事。你曾經想寫一本小說嗎？每年11月，成千上萬的人嘗試從頭開始寫自己的5萬字小說，結果發現，你所要做的就是每天寫1667字順便說一下，秘訣就是不睡覺

angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.en: until you've written your words for the day. You might be sleep-deprived, but you'll finish your novel. Now is my book the next great American novel? No. I wrote it in a month. But for the rest of my life, if I meet John Hodgman at a TED party, I don't have to say, "I'm a computer scientist."
angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.zh: 直到你寫完當天的話。你可能睡眠不足，但你會完成你的小說。現在我的書會是下一部偉大的美國小說嗎？不，我花了一個月的時間寫的。但在我的餘生中，如果我在 TED 聚會上遇到約翰·霍奇曼，我不必說：“我是一名計算機科學家。”

angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.en: No, no, if I want to, I can say, "I'm a novelist." So here's one last thing I'd like to mention. I learned that when I made small, sustainable changes, things I could keep doing, they were more likely to stick. There's nothing wrong with big, crazy challenges. In fact, they're a ton of fun.
angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.zh: 不，不，如果我願意，我可以說：「我是小說家。」這是我想提的最後一件事。我了解到，當我做出小的、可持續的改變時，我可以繼續做的事情，它們更有可能堅持下去。巨大而瘋狂的挑戰並沒有什麼不好。事實上，它們非常有趣。

angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.en: But they're less likely to stick. When I gave up sugar for 30 days, day 31 looked like this. So here's my question to you: What are you waiting for? I guarantee you the next 30 days are going to pass whether you like it or not, so why not think about something you have always wanted to try
angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.zh: 但他們不太可能堅持下去。當我戒糖 30 天時，第 31 天是這樣的。所以這是我問你的問題：你還在等什麼？我向你保證，不管你喜歡與否，接下來的30天都會過去，所以為什麼不想想你一直想嘗試的事情呢？

angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.en: and give it a shot! For the next 30 days. Notifications alt+T
angela_lee_duckworth_grit_the_power_of_p.zh: 並試一試！接下來的 30 天。通知 alt+T
